architecture.summary: Turn-averaged SAEs modify standard sparse autoencoder architectures to operate on aggregated activations across an entire conversational turn (Human or Assistant speech) rather than individual tokens. The system learns to reconstruct the mean of model activations across a turn using a fixed set of learned features, reducing feature count from linear-in-context-length to constant-per-turn. The SAE comprises an encoder that maps the average activation to a sparse latent representation, and a decoder that reconstructs the turn-average activation from this sparse code.

biblio.title: Turn-Averaged SAEs for Feature Discovery and Long-Context Attribution

claims.statement: turn-averaged features describe a single turn's high-level characteristics more completely than per-token features when judged by an LLM
turn-averaged SAEs greatly simplify common downstream uses of SAEs like attribution graphs
the number of active features scales linearly with context length in standard SAE architectures, whereas turn-averaged SAEs use a fixed number of features per turn
